author: @kamathlaxminarayana301
text:
so, you are used to playing with high voltages/amperes, etc. . Why don't you build yourself a scanning electron microscope like this guy did? https://youtu.be/sG7G3LN_SN8

Might prove usefull for so many projects, as many of your experiments revolve around nano structures.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I only have so much time mate lol

replies.author: @kamathlaxminarayana301
replies.text:
:) Or of course, there are many of them on ebay  second hand. Just had a feeling that someone who plays at the nano scale must be able to see at the nano scale.

Well, you are a scientist , and as all good scientists do, you have an awesome imagination and calculation capabilities in your head, and may be you are letting it be your microscope.! Cheers :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: if i need to mat - i send it out for SEM imaging - but it is £2,500 every time i do so i tend to be a bit restrictive on what i get imaged - but this channel is meant for the home scientist and not everybody knows how to get that doen or can afford it

replies.author: @kamathlaxminarayana301
replies.text: Oh Ok! Thanks . now I get the drift!

replies.author: @kamathlaxminarayana301
replies.text:
This is a Scanning Tunneling (as opposed to Electron beam) Microscope made from probably somewhere between hundred euros to a thousand euros max worth of parts.  
https://dberard.com/home-built-stm/
Anyone with enough DIY experience of making a 3d printer on their own might be able to pull this one off. 
Robert: are images from STMs as opposed to SEMs usefull for supercapacitor/battery research?

Of course, you might still not have enough time to build this, but leaving a link here for anyone interested (stumbled upon this yesterday when looking for something totally unrelated  :)  )

Can't even think! Fast charging with charge stations equipped with those batteries to give the needed power. A dream!

author: @humanglitch1977
text: Its nice to see you working on phase five but as a consumer, when can I buy a phase one battery?. say for a quadcopter for example.  This would be a good real world test for high discharge. just watch a fpv drone racer. Our flight batteries lasts just 3-5 mins and require very high c ratings. please continue your work in helping to save the planet :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: when we get it into production mate and we are in discussion about that now

author: @Barskor1
text: That cage will stop most shrapnel but a pressure wave can still injure or kill, explosive storage directs those forces to safer areas like up or open empty ground to a side. Be safe and Merry Christmass.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: merry xmas too

author: @giannagiavelli5098
text: Gorgeous pocket watch you are becoming our modern Tesla

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - I always dress that way out of work - I am going to a prize giving tonight and don't have time to go home and change

replies.author: @giannagiavelli5098
replies.text: ah we catch a glimpse. I am a big horology fan as a tinkerer myself but I lost my beautiful german pocket watch and haven't yet gotten another.  I did just aquire the new rubber banded black ringed rose gold rolex diver watch and its meeting my needs. a pretty piece I just love that they put black over all the gold to make it less ostentacious.  Well we would love you to make our power source for our neural cubes we would need just a few as we ramp up as demos to vulture capitalists. our target goal is about 700 watts at 12 volts for 18 hours. witha  size of 6"x6"xX so we would need to know what X would be.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the size i normally make is 9 cm square by 0.1mm thick with 0.05g of material and it works out to be around 8mWh or so - so I am guessing 6" x 6" by 0.016" would do it

replies.author: @giannagiavelli5098
replies.text: thanks!

replies.author: @almartel1416
replies.text: Ahem... Tesla 2.0 ;-}}

author: @solarhope
text:
Hi Rob,
Good couple of vids on HTC.
I have been using the 25ml device (around the £30 mark) for the last few months, it does the job, but not the best quality.
I think I overfilled it on the last sugar experiment, and I had one hell of a game getting back into it again with stilsons and a blow lamp :-(
Merry Christmas to you and yours - Martin.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - i am sorry mate i shouldn't laugh - i did that too but had to cut the top off with an angle grinder then buy another one!!
